context.problemStatement: The completed standing Bible corpus audit validates JSON/schema/chapter/verse structure, but it does not validate canonical expected verse counts for each book/chapter. Severe missing target verses were exposed by Rotherham source/target alignment checks.
context.failedAssumption: The project treated the prior structural audit as if it were a full Bible corpus completeness audit. That was incorrect.

requiredNextWork.title: Add standing canonical Bible verse-count completeness audit
requiredNextWork.description: Add a quiet audit that checks each ordinary Bible book/chapter against a governed canonical expected verse-count table or explicitly governed exception list.
mustPassBefore: any claim that the Bible corpus is complete

requiredNextWork.title: Identify legitimate source/rights basis for the existing target translation

notAuthorized: Do not repair target translation text from Rotherham.
Do not add placeholders for missing target verses.
Do not blind-import Rotherham into blocker books.
Do not describe the Bible corpus audit as complete until canonical expected verse-count auditing exists and passes.
